§3.5 Difference and Sum Matrices

Definition 3.5.1.

A difference matrix is a square matrix where

When acted upon a vector , ie. , each element in the resulting vector represents the difference between the corresponding element in and the previous element, with the exception of the first element, which remains the same. Formally,

, where .

Conversely, define the sum matrix as the following.

Definition 3.5.2.

A sum matrix is a square matrix where

Then, for a vector , is a vector where each element in the resulting vector is the sum of all the elements in up to the corresponding element,

Theorem 3.5.3.

Let be the difference matrix with rows and be the sum matrix with rows, then , ie. they are inverses of each other.

By visualizing the vector to be dense as a function, say ,

and

.

This viewpoint provides another way to look at the relationship between differentiation and integration.

Furthermore, by using rules on matrices, we are able to see properties of differentiation an integration in a linear algebra way.

Definition 3.5.4.

Define the inner product of two functions , and , to be
